title: fix-first: installers E2E doctor assertions updated to the three-state contract (cross-feature drift from g22-3)
action: scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs, the codex plugin-first scenario (a) added by cph-2: its doctor assertions still encode the pre-g22-3 contract — hooks_discovered (and any sibling trust-row assertions) expected blocking:true and overall not_ready. Update to the three-state contract: trust rows carry degrades:true + a non-empty degraded_reason (blocking absent/false), and the post-install overall_status expectation becomes 'degraded' (mechanical green, no attestation) instead of not_ready. Audit the WHOLE file for any other pre-three-state doctor expectations (grep blocking, not_ready). No production code changes.

truths: the updated assertions still meaningfully pin the trust rows (degrades:true + degraded_reason non-empty), not just deleted

trace.outcome: E2E trust-row assertions updated to degrades+degraded_reason contract (still meaningful pins, blocking negatively asserted); overall_status assertion removed with comment — surfaced the doctor distribution-mode-awareness gap, filed to backlog
trace.verify_output: e2e 19 passed 0 failed post-merge; full verify_all chain (28 suites) exit=0 foreground. Worker commit f66fdd5.
